Phase-42.NAME: MTProto Usage Analytics and Promotion Tag Control
Phase-42.purpose: Give the KrotPN operator metadata-only MTProto usage analytics, abuse visibility, runtime availability proof, and safe @MTProxyBot promotion tag control without exposing proxy secrets or Telegram payload data.

scope.in: Usage events, sessions, rollups, last seen, traffic windows, errors, unknown/rejected SNI, active connections, top users, observe-only abuse signals, runtime health, req_pq proof, admin API, admin UI, and promotion tag control.
scope.out: Telegram chat/channel/message visibility, Telegram account inspection, payload inspection, automatic punitive blocking, deploy/install redesign, and user-facing proxy reissue unless explicitly required.

privacy-boundary.rule-1: MTProto analytics is transport metadata only.
privacy-boundary.rule-2: Do not attempt to infer Telegram chats, channels, message content, contacts, or Telegram user ids.
privacy-boundary.rule-3: Raw IP storage is avoided by default; use HMAC/hash or aggregate prefixes for distinct-client analytics.

source-notes.note-1: Telegram MTProxy promotion tags are obtained through @MTProxyBot and configured on the server side; see https://core.telegram.org/proxy.
source-notes.note-2: Existing KrotPN production path remains Phase-41 KPprotoN fake-TLS, not the superseded official MTProxy path.

steps.step-1: Design Alembic-backed usage event, session, rollup, last-seen, and abuse-signal tables with secret-free fields and bounded retention.
steps.step-2: Extend KPprotoN runtime policy metadata, mtproto_proxy metric backend, active-SNI sampler, and private API to expose handshake, close, bytes, error, unknown_sni, rejected_sni, active connection, and req_pq proof telemetry.
steps.step-3: Add backend telemetry ingestion task with cursoring, idempotent event writes, failure-safe retries, and no impact on MTProto availability.
steps.step-4: Build aggregation service for global summary, per-proxy usage, top users, day/week/month traffic, session duration, connection count, and last seen.
steps.step-5: Add observe-only abuse classifiers for many IP hashes, high concurrent sessions, traffic spikes, and repeated handshake failures.
steps.step-6: Expose admin analytics APIs with filters, pagination, redaction guards, and per-assignment drill-down.
steps.step-7: Add compact frontend-admin analytics UI with mobile-safe summary, time filters, top users, per-proxy detail, abuse signal lists, and runtime proof status.
steps.step-8: Implement promotion tag validation, masked storage/state, explicit admin update, audit logging, and runtime apply or pending-restart semantics.
steps.step-9: Propagate non-blank MTPROTO_AD_TAG/PROXY_AD_TAG to the DE runtime without rotating user SNI, base secret, salt, or proxy links.
steps.step-10: Run backend, runtime smoke, admin UI build, redaction smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and live optional req_pq proof before release.

data-flow.flow-1: Telegram client -> RU SNI router -> DE KPprotoN runtime -> Telegram DC.
data-flow.flow-2: DE runtime emits secret-free telemetry -> backend ingestion task -> Postgres M-054 tables -> M-056 aggregation -> M-057 admin API -> M-058 admin UI.
data-flow.flow-3: Admin promotion tag update -> M-059 validation/storage/audit -> runtime env/apply state -> M-057/M-058 masked status.

phase-gates.gate-1: All telemetry payloads and admin responses pass redaction checks.
phase-gates.gate-2: Runtime telemetry buffer overflow cannot break MTProto traffic.
phase-gates.gate-3: Admin can view global and per-proxy stats for issued, active, disabled, reissue_required, active connections, last seen, traffic, errors, sessions, top users, and abuse signals.
phase-gates.gate-4: Promotion tag accepts valid 32-hex tag or zero fallback, rejects blanks/malformed values, and never logs full tag.
phase-gates.gate-5: Existing Phase-41 issued-link req_pq and Telegram Desktop/mobile proof standard remains the release bar for MTProto edge changes.

risks.risk-1: Exact byte/session counters may require careful hooks inside or around mtproto_proxy internals.
risks.risk-2: Telemetry volume can grow quickly; bounded raw event retention and rollups are required.
risks.risk-3: Mobile networks may trigger false abuse signals; Phase-42 remains observe-only.
risks.risk-4: Promotion tag updates may require controlled runtime restart if hot-apply is unavailable.
risks.risk-5: Live byte counters are exact globally; exact per-user byte attribution during concurrent active SNI domains still requires a deeper data-plane hook or live validation before enforcement use.

verification-design.gate-1: Runtime telemetry must become idempotent database evidence without blocking MTProto traffic or storing secrets.
verification-design.gate-2: Analytics must expose issued totals, status counts, active connections, last seen, traffic windows, errors, session duration, top users, abuse signals, runtime health, and req_pq proof state.
verification-design.gate-3: Admin API and UI must be admin-only, compact on mobile, filterable, paginated where needed, and redaction-guarded.
verification-design.gate-4: Promotion tag control must accept valid 32-hex @MTProxyBot tags or zero fallback, reject blanks/malformed values, and never log or render the full tag.
verification-design.gate-5: Any runtime change must preserve Phase-41 issued-link req_pq and Telegram Desktop/mobile availability proof.

evidence-levels.level-1: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and documentation/index consistency for Phase-42 artifacts.
evidence-levels.level-2: Local deterministic backend tests for M-054, M-056, M-057, and M-059.
evidence-levels.level-3: Local runtime smoke for M-055 metric backend, active-SNI sampler, private telemetry API, and promotion tag propagation using synthetic telemetry and fake tags.
evidence-levels.level-4: Frontend-admin build plus static/mobile smoke for M-058.
evidence-levels.level-5: Optional live proof after operator deploy: issued-link mtp_ping/req_pq, Telegram Desktop/mobile availability, and masked admin analytics inspection.

redaction-gates.gate-1: No full tg links, fake-TLS secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, private keys, raw public IPs, or full promotion tags in API responses, logs, telemetry, audit rows, or frontend bundles.
redaction-gates.gate-2: Telegram chats, channels, messages, contacts, Telegram account ids, or payload content are out of scope and must not be inferred.
redaction-gates.gate-3: Unknown/rejected SNI events are retained only with masked identifiers and safe reason codes.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-42.xml docs/modules/M-054.xml docs/modules/M-055.xml docs/modules/M-056.xml docs/modules/M-057.xml docs/modules/M-058.xml docs/modules/M-059.xml docs/verification/V-M-054.xml docs/verification/V-M-055.xml docs/verification/V-M-056.xml docs/verification/V-M-057.xml docs/verification/V-M-058.xml docs/verification/V-M-059.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/current-status.xml

cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_usage_telemetry.py tests/test_mtproto_runtime_telemetry_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_analytics_service.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_analytics_api.py tests/test_mtproto_promotion_tag.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py -v

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Documentation, local unit tests, static smoke scripts, frontend build, and synthetic runtime fixtures are safe to execute autonomously.
autonomous-execution-safety.blocked-live: Production runtime restart, real promotion tag apply, retention pruning, or live traffic inspection require explicit operator approval and redacted evidence only.
